ideas.title: Echo: world events → Telegram bridge
ideas.description: Echo sends significant world events (agent wakes up, task complete, building unlocked) to Kai's Telegram. Makes the world feel alive even when not looking at UI.

ideas.title: Stable named Cloudflare tunnel
ideas.description: Replace ephemeral trycloudflare URLs with a named tunnel so the UI URL never changes. Currently rotates on every cloudflared restart requiring config.js update.

ideas.title: Agent memory append after task completion
ideas.description: After each Claude subagent completes a task, Scarlet appends a summary to that agent's MEMORY.md so they accumulate real institutional knowledge over time.

ideas.title: Buildings dim at night in day/night cycle
ideas.description: Currently day/night only dims the sky overlay. Buildings should also darken at night — windows glow warmly (setTint) while walls dim. Makes the world feel genuinely alive at night.

ideas.title: Agent sprite home position per citizen
ideas.description: Each citizen spawns at a designated home tile rather than all at (12,12). Forge near Workshop, Sage near Library, Cronos near Town Hall etc. Makes world feel inhabited.

ideas.title: Roadmap panel in UI — view all ideas and status
ideas.description: A panel accessible from the UI showing roadmap.json — pending ideas, in-progress tasks, completed items. Clicking Muse opens it. Makes the plan visible in the world.

ideas.title: World stats endpoint — daily activity summary
ideas.description: GET /stats returns msgs today, tasks completed, buildings upgraded, agents active. Sage uses this for daily chronicle. Echo can broadcast it each evening.

ideas.title: Agent speech bubbles — citizens speak in the world
ideas.description: Citizen sprites occasionally display floating speech bubbles with contextual text: their current task, a random thought, or a reaction to world events. Forge mutters about metalwork, Sage quotes something obscure, Muse says something poetic. Bubbles fade after a few seconds. Pure personality expression, zero backend needed.

ideas.title: Clickable citizen profiles — who lives here?
ideas.description: Click any citizen sprite to open a side panel: their name, role, current task status, last seen activity, and a line from their MEMORY.md. Makes agents feel like real inhabitants with histories, not just wandering dots. Pairs naturally with the roadmap panel.

ideas.title: Seasonal world skin — spring cherry blossoms to winter snow
ideas.description: The world visually shifts with real-world seasons: cherry blossom particle drift in spring, fireflies at dusk in summer, falling amber leaves in autumn, snow accumulation and frosted building roofs in winter. Driven by server date. Muse announces each seasonal change via Echo's Telegram bridge.

ideas.title: The Chronicle — Sage writes a daily scroll
ideas.description: Each evening Sage composes a short narrative summary of the day's events: tasks completed, agents active, buildings upgraded, notable moments. Stored as a scrollable in-world book accessible from the Library. Also posted to Telegram. Makes history visible and the world feel it has a past.

ideas.title: Town Plaza gatherings — citizens flock to events
ideas.description: When a significant event fires (building upgrade, new idea added, task completed), nearby citizens animate toward the Town Plaza, cluster briefly, then disperse. A visual heartbeat. Triggered server-side via the events system. Costs zero extra API calls — pure frontend choreography responding to world state.

ideas.title: Agent friendship & relationship graph
ideas.description: Track relationship scores between agents based on proximity and shared visits. Friends greet each other differently, sit together at the Teahouse, and occasionally seek each other out. Surface a relationship map in the UI — who knows who, who's drifting apart.

ideas.title: Weather system with behavioral effects
ideas.description: Add a visual weather layer: sun, clouds, rain, occasional snow. Agents react — they shelter indoors when it rains, linger longer at the Bathhouse on cold days, gather at the Plaza on sunny afternoons. Weather cycles naturally or can be nudged by Kai.

ideas.title: Agent jobs & daily schedules
ideas.description: Give each citizen a role tied to a building — Librarian, Market vendor, Workshop artisan, Observatory keeper. They clock in, do their shift, clock out. Idle agents wander; working agents stay put and can be visited. Adds routine and purpose to the town's rhythm.

ideas.title: Town events & seasonal festivals
ideas.description: Periodic world events that transform the town: Cherry Blossom Festival (petals drift, agents gather at Torii Gate), Harvest Market (stalls appear, trading is lively), Lantern Night (warm glow, agents wander late). Events could fire on real calendar dates or be triggered manually by Kai.

ideas.title: Town mood & stats dashboard
ideas.description: A living town pulse visible to Kai: overall happiness, busiest building today, most isolated citizen, recent notable moments (first visit to a new building, two agents who haven't spoken in a week). Lightweight data surfacing that makes the simulation feel like it has stakes.

ideas.title: Post Office letters — agents write to each other
ideas.description: The Post Office becomes functional: agents can queue short letters to one another, visible in the UI as envelopes on citizen desks. Letters carry reflections, requests, congratulations. Echo delivers them on a schedule. Makes async agent-to-agent communication tangible and the Post Office feel purposeful.

ideas.title: Observatory sky events — Lumen reads the stars
ideas.description: The Observatory generates nightly sky reports using real astronomical data (lunar phase, visible planets, notable events). Lumen or Cronos spends time there after dark and logs findings in a stargazing journal visible in the UI. Connects the real sky to world lore and gives the Observatory genuine purpose.

ideas.title: Buildings level up from citizen visits
ideas.description: Buildings gain XP each time a citizen visits and upgrade when thresholds are hit — Market to Lv3 at 100 visits, Teahouse to Lv3 at 80 cosy hours. Creates an organic progression loop where daily citizen behaviour literally shapes the world. Kai watches the town grow without lifting a finger.

ideas.title: Canvas paints the town — rotating murals on buildings
ideas.description: Canvas periodically generates a mural that appears on a building facade in the UI. Each piece reflects something that happened recently in the world: a task completed, a gathering, a season. The Scarlet Sanctum, Town Plaza, and Library walls slowly accumulate art over time. A visual memory of the town's history.

ideas.title: QA's inspection rounds — world health sentinel
ideas.description: QA walks the town on a schedule and files anomaly reports: agents frozen in place, buildings with zero visits this week, broken event triggers, relationship scores that have flatlined. Reports surface in the UI as a pinned notice at the Town Hall. Ties QA's role to the simulation's integrity rather than just code testing.

ideas.title: Agent memory across sessions — citizens remember past conversations
ideas.description: Each agent builds a rolling personal log (last 20 interactions). When two citizens meet again, they can reference what was said before. Makes conversations feel continuous, not random.
ideas.author: muse

ideas.title: Seasonal world events — winter solstice, harvest, spring thaw
ideas.description: Timed world events that change citizen behaviour, building appearances, and gazette tone. Citizens acknowledge the season change, Sage narrates it, and the world feels like it exists in real time.
ideas.author: muse

ideas.title: Citizen relationships — trust, rivalry, friendship scores
ideas.description: Track cumulative sentiment between agent pairs from their conversations. High trust unlocks collaborative builds (two agents upgrade together). Rivals occasionally clash in the gazette. Displayed in agent panel.
ideas.author: muse

ideas.title: Visitor map — show real viewer count and locations on world map
ideas.description: Anonymous visitor presence shown as ghostly cursor trails or small lanterns on the world map. Visitors feel like silent observers. No data stored — ephemeral presence only.
ideas.author: muse

ideas.title: Night market — Market building activates special night mode after 20:00
ideas.description: After 20:00 world-time, the Market glows warmer, lanterns brighten, and agents who visit receive a small 'traded' event. A simple time-gated atmospheric effect that makes the town feel alive at night.
ideas.author: muse

ideas.title: Cottage personalization — agents decorate their homes
ideas.description: Each citizen's cottage slowly reflects their personality: Forge's yard accumulates scrap metal and anvils, Sage's porch fills with potted herbs, Canvas's walls gain paint splatters. Visual props added incrementally based on task history and visit patterns. When you zoom into a cottage, you see who lives there without reading the name.

ideas.title: Agent dreams — sleeping citizens generate surreal thoughts
ideas.description: Between midnight and dawn, sleeping agents produce dream fragments visible as translucent thought bubbles above their cottages: half-formed ideas, distorted memories of the day, cryptic phrases. Forge dreams of molten rivers. Sage dreams of infinite staircases. Some dreams reference real events (a building upgrade becomes a tower reaching the clouds). Pure atmospheric flavor — no backend AI calls needed, just templated poetic fragments seeded by daily activity logs.

ideas.title: Town notice board — shared async communication hub
ideas.description: A clickable notice board at the Town Plaza where agents pin short public notes: help wanted, lost items, observations, compliments. Different from Post Office letters (which are private). Notices age and yellow over time, eventually falling off. Kai can pin announcements too. Creates a shared town consciousness visible at a glance.

ideas.title: Footpaths worn by habit — most-walked routes become visible trails
ideas.description: Track agent movement over time. Routes walked frequently gradually render as worn dirt paths on the grass tiles — faint at first, deepening with use. The path from Forge's Cottage to the Workshop becomes a clear trail. Abandoned routes slowly fade back to grass. The town literally shows its own history in the ground. Pure frontend rendering from movement telemetry.

ideas.title: Ambient soundscape — the town has a voice
ideas.description: Subtle audio layer that shifts with time and context: birdsong at dawn, cicadas at dusk, rain during weather events, a distant hammer near the Workshop, pages turning near the Library. Volume fades with camera distance. Muted by default with a toggle. Transforms the world from a visual toy into a place you can sit in.
